meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Solving Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Learn to implement professional SaaS pricing while solving version control issues. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: To solve the chaos of unversioned SaaS pricing iterations, you must treat your pricing logic as code. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative deployment, you establish a single source of truth. This ensures your pricing models are versioned, testable, and instantly deployable to production.
content.intro:
The midnight sweat hits when you realize your client's pricing logic is scattered across three different spreadsheet versions, a half-baked Notion doc, and a 'final_v2_real.js' file in your root directory. Without version control, your SaaS builds are fragile, prone to catastrophic regression, and impossible to audit when the revenue numbers don't match the dashboard.

You aren't just building a feature; you are building a financial engine. By treating your SaaS pricing logic as a version-controlled entity, you transform from a reactive coder into a proactive architect. This guide shows you how to use modern AI-assisted tools to automate the technical debt away, letting you ship professional, auditable pricing structures in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish a structured JSON schema for your pricing tiers to ensure data consistency across your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing object including fields for 'tier_name', 'monthly_price', 'features_array', and 'stripe_price_id'. Ensure strict typing for currency and billing intervals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file pricing_schema.ts --task 'Generate Zod validation schemas for the pricing interface'

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Storage
steps.description: Create a dedicated directory for pricing configurations to track changes via Git, preventing the 'no version control' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a directory structure /config/pricing/ with versioned files like 'v1_alpha.json'. Include a README that explains how to document price changes.
steps.claude_code_command: mkdir -p config/pricing && git add config/pricing && git commit -m 'Initialize pricing version control'

steps.title: Implement Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Inject your pricing logic into the codebase using Claude Code to ensure it integrates seamlessly with your existing billing flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service function 'getPriceForTier(tierName: string)' that imports the latest version from /config/pricing and handles fallback logic for deprecated tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Refactor the pricing service to dynamically load the latest JSON config from the /config/pricing directory'

steps.title: Automate Price Testing
steps.description: Create unit tests to verify that pricing calculations remain accurate across different versions of your configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test cases for the pricing service that verify price calculations for different tiers and ensure the system correctly identifies the 'latest' version.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test --task 'Run unit tests for pricing service and ensure 100% coverage on price calculation logic'

steps.title: Deploy with Rollback Capability
steps.description: Finalize the workflow by ensuring any pricing update is a single commit, making rollbacks instantaneous if a bug is detected.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates the JSON schema of the pricing config before allowing a push to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a pre-push git hook that validates pricing JSON files against the Zod schema'

pro_tips: Always store pricing IDs from your payment processor (Stripe/LemonSqueezy) in your JSON config, never hardcode them in the UI.
Use a 'deprecated' flag in your pricing JSON to allow for soft-sunsetting of old tiers without breaking historical user data.
Implement a 'pricing-preview' environment variable to test new tiers in production without affecting live customers.
Treat your pricing config as a public-facing API endpoint; keep the structure flat to allow easy consumption by both frontend and backend.
Use semantic versioning (e.g., v1.2.0) for your pricing files to track the evolution of your business strategy alongside your code.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is version-controlled, integrate it with a feature-flagging service to allow for A/B testing different pricing models in real-time.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unmanaged code dictate your revenue strategy. By centralizing and versioning your pricing logic, you reclaim control over your SaaS business and build with the confidence of a lead architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
